headline: Full request context in the audit log and an Audit Log settings panel
summary: Audit entries now record the full who / what / when / where / how of each action, with an admin panel to control IP visibility, user-agent capture, and retention.

items.title: Full request context on every audit entry
items.body: Audit entries now record the request method and path, the authentication type (session, API key, or anonymous), the client user-agent (when enabled), and a request id, alongside the existing performer, target, and source IP. The audit viewer groups each entry into a who / what / when / where / how breakdown on click, and the search box now also matches on IP, path, and user-agent. The added fields that carry identifiers (path, user-agent) are encrypted at rest and shown decrypted to admins.

items.title: Audit Log settings panel
items.body: A new admin Settings tab controls the audit log: set a retention period in days or keep entries indefinitely; record full IP addresses for investigations (off by default — otherwise the source IP is stored as a one-way hash the operator cannot reverse); and capture the client user-agent. The IP and user-agent settings apply to new entries only — values already stored as a hash stay private.
